pages.full_text:
The Abbey Dore building was started in the reign of Stephen 1135-54, on land which was then in the parish of Bocton, given to the Cistercians by a grandson of William I. The order had been founded in Citeaux in Burgundy in 1128. Its aims were simplicity & austerity: Farming rather than learning.
	Dore came from the Welsh "dour," water. Abbey by the water.
	At the Abbey was a woman serving coffee, who said that she had grandchildren at Blue Coat. She is
pages.notes: Page number not visible in top-left corner; text continues from previous spread. 'Bocton' may be a variant spelling of Bacton (parish in Herefordshire). 'Blue Coat' likely refers to a school (Blue Coat School). A photograph of the abbey interior is glued in; '4' below it appears to be a photo caption number, not a diary page number. Text is cut off mid-sentence at the bottom.

pages.full_text:
__Screen__

THIS IS A VERY FINE piece of Renaissance woodwork, almost certainly the work of John Abel, 1577-64. Surviving works which are

Jo McDonald's mother!
	Apparently Charles I's arms are in the middle of the screen, & the arms of the family who stole the monastery - Scudamore & another, are [in] place of the thieves!
	Beautiful stonework, but much of it has disappeared. They were having a [G]regorian Chant workshop day.
	We left after lunch: or rather we
pages.notes: Page number at top-right corner is partially cut off and unclear — possibly '35' or similar. A glued-in printed postcard/leaflet about the Screen at Abbey Dore is present; the printed text is partially visible ('THIS IS A VERY FINE piece of Renaissance woodwork, almost certainly the work of John Abel, 1577-64. Surviving works which are' — text is cut off). '6' below the postcard image appears to be a photo caption number. 'Gregorian' is inferred from '[G]regorian' where leading G is somewhat obscured. 'another' after 'Scudamore &' — the second family name is not given, diarist wrote 'another'. Text cut off mid-sentence at bottom. '1577-64' in printed text is as written (likely '1577-1664' or similar — not corrected per verbatim rules).
